body:
{
  "title": "Indigo Ferreira — Head of Security, Anchor Codec",
  "compiled_truth": "Indigo Ferreira is the Head of Security at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor), where she oversees all aspects of security engineering, threat modeling, and cryptographic assurance for the company's deterministic data infrastructure. She joined in August 2027, roughly eighteen months into Anchor's arc, at a moment when the company was scaling its single-pass primitives into production environments that demanded rigorous security guarantees.

Before Anchor, Indigo spent nearly a decade in applied cryptography and security engineering. She's known for advising three separate infrastructure companies on post-quantum PKI migration — work that gave her an unusually deep understanding of how cryptographic transitions ripple through real-world systems. People who've worked with her describe her as a first-principles thinker: someone who refuses to accept inherited assumptions about trust boundaries or key management without re-deriving the reasoning from scratch. This occasionally makes her slow to commit to a design, but the designs she does commit to tend to hold up under adversarial scrutiny.

Her hiring at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) was seen internally as a signal that the company was entering a new phase — one where security couldn't remain the part-time concern of the core engineering team. Indigo's initial focus was on auditing Anchor's authentication layer and establishing a formal threat model that accounted for both classical and post-quantum attack surfaces. By late 2027 she had restructured the security review process and brought on two additional engineers to her team.

Indigo is technically deep but not particularly interested in the spotlight. She's given a handful of talks at smaller security conferences, mostly on the practical challenges of migrating infrastructure to post-quantum schemes — the kind of talks that get circulated in security Slacks rather than making headlines. Her writing tends to be terse, precise, sometimes almost curt. Internally she's known for sending one-line code review comments that somehow convey paragraphs of context.

She holds a master's in applied mathematics from a mid-tier university that she rarely mentions. Outside of work, she's reportedly an avid rock climber, though no one on the team has actually seen photographic evidance of this.",
  "timeline": "- **2026-11-15** | Indigo Ferreira gives a talk at a small post-quantum cryptography workshop on practical PKI migration failures — the talk circulates widely in security circles and catches the attention of several infrastructure startups.
- **2027-03-22** | Initial conversation between Indigo and [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) leadership about the Head of Security role; she reportedly spent two hours grilling the engineering team on their trust model before agreeing to a second call.
- **2027-06-10** | Indigo completes advisory engagement with her third infrastructure client on post-quantum PKI migration, freeing her to consider a full-time position.
- **2027-08-01** | Officially joins [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) as Head of Security.
- **2027-09-14** | Completes first full threat model audit of Anchor's single-pass authentication layer; circulates a 40-page internal doc that reportedly rewrote several assumptions the team had carried since 2026.
- **2027-11-03** | Hires first dedicated security engineer to her team, expanding the security function beyond a one-person operation.
- **2027-12-18** | Presents internal findings on post-quantum readiness of Anchor's primitives to the full engineering org; the session runs two hours over its scheduled time.
- **2028-02-07** | Second security engineer joins Indigo's team, completing the initial buildout of the security group.
- **2028-04-22** | Indigo leads Anchor's first external security review engagement with a third-party audit firm, coordinating disclosure and remediation timelines.
- **2028-06-11** | Speaks on a panel about deterministic security guarantees at an infrastructure-focused conference; her remarks on "trust boundary hygeine" are widely quoted in post-event writeups."
}
